thesis: MARA is one of the largest publicly traded Bitcoin miners, with 1.7 GW of captive power, 72.2 EH/s energized hashrate as of Q1 2026, and a year-end 2025 BTC treasury of 53,822 BTC (~$4.7B). Q1 2026 reported a $1.26B net loss driven by a ~$1.0B non-cash Bitcoin fair-value impairment, and the company sold 20,880 BTC for $1.5B to fund operations and reduce total convertible debt from ~$3.3B to ~$2.3B. The AI/HPC pivot via the Starwood Capital partnership remains active but nascent. At $12.40, MARA trades near the lower end of analyst consensus and offers asymmetric upside to a BTC rally, AI/HPC milestones, or further deleveraging, while carrying high beta and mark-to-market volatility.

financials.revenue_growth: 38% YoY in FY2025; -18% YoY in Q1 2026
financials.fcf: Negative during expansion; Q1 2026 BTC sale raised $1.5B to fund operations and reduce debt

drivers: Bitcoin price rallies and risk appetite returns to crypto equities
AI/HPC pivot monetizes via Starwood partnership with signed contracts and deployed racks
Further debt reduction and balance-sheet de-risking

risks: Bitcoin bear market below key support erodes NAV and mining margins
Remaining ~$2.3B convertible debt creates dilution and refinancing pressure
AI pivot delayed or fails to secure contracts; additional treasury sales needed

base_case.assumptions: Bitcoin stable to moderately higher; operational execution on hashrate and fleet efficiency; modest AI infrastructure contribution by 2027; analyst consensus median around $13.00–$18.38

catalysts.event: Hashrate progress, AI infrastructure update, treasury strategy
Block reward cut to 1.5625 BTC — long-term structural headwind

catalysts.date: Strategic AI/HPC partnership milestones / data center deployments
catalysts.event: Starwood Capital partnership milestones and HPC rack deployments
Potential U.S. crypto policy tailwinds and Strategic Bitcoin Reserve developments

key_risks: Single-asset concentration: 90%+ value tied to Bitcoin price and high equity beta
Mining margin compression from global hashrate growth and Bitcoin halving in 2028
Mark-to-market volatility: BTC fair-value impairments can overwhelm operating results
Convertible debt overhang (~$2.3B post-Q1 2026) creating dilution and refinancing risk
Regulatory risk (SEC, environmental, energy policy) and potential crypto crackdowns
